records.ety: [F. reptile, L. reptilis, fr. repere, reptum, to creep; cf. Lith. reploti; perh. akin to L. serpere. Cf. Serpent.]

defs: Creeping; moving on the belly, or by means of small and short legs.
Hence: Groveling; low; vulgar; as, a reptile race or crew; reptile vices.
quotes: There is also a false, reptile prudence, the result not of caution, but of fear.
And dislodge their reptile souls From the bodies and forms of men.

defs: An animal that crawls, or moves on its belly, as snakes,, or by means of small, short legs, as lizards, and the like.
quotes: An inadvertent step may crush the snail That crawls at evening in the public path; But he that has humanity, forewarned, Will tread aside, and let the reptile live.

defs: One of the Reptilia, or one of the Amphibia.
notes: ☞ The amphibians were formerly classed with Reptilia, and are still popularly called reptiles, though much more closely allied to the fishes.

defs: A groveling or very mean person.
